examples.title: Basic Dictionary Word Counter
examples.description: A program that counts the total number of words in the Unix dictionary file
examples.explanation: This program opens the system dictionary file, reads it line by line using fgets(), counts each line (word), and prints the total count. It demonstrates basic file handling with proper error checking and resource cleanup.

examples.title: Dictionary Word Lookup
examples.description: Program to check if a given word exists in the dictionary file
examples.explanation: This program performs a linear search through the dictionary file to find a word. It converts both the input word and dictionary words to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison. The newline is removed using strcspn() before comparison.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) in worst case, where n is the number of words in dictionary

examples.title: Hash Table Based Dictionary for Fast Lookup
examples.description: Preloads dictionary into a hash table for O(1) average-case word lookup
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates an efficient dictionary lookup using a hash table. It preloads the entire dictionary into memory (O(n) loading time), then provides O(1) average-case lookup for subsequent word searches. This is ideal for applications that need to check many words against the dictionary.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) to load dictionary, O(1) average for each lookup
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing dictionary in hash table
